svg.aria-label: Sequence comparison with cleanup: inline PDF re-ships and re-parses every call; Files API uploads once and references a tiny file_id; the stored file is removed either by an explicit DELETE call or automatically by a server-side expires_after timer.
svg.text: OpenAI — inline PDF vs Files API: traffic, then cleanup
A · Inline PDF — type: input_file, file_data (base64)
Behind the scenes (every call): decode base64 → render each page to an image + extract text → model reads both.
The whole document crosses the wire and is re-parsed on every single query.
B · Files API — upload once, reference by file_id
Stored & parsed once — OpenAI keeps the parsed file server-side.
Behind the scenes: the file is already stored and parsed — OpenAI just looks it up. No re-upload, no re-parse, tiny request.
Removing the stored file — two ways it goes away
⑦ DELETE /v1/files/{id} · explicit, from your app
expires_after / TTL elapses (server deletes it, no call)
Same parsing happens either way — the question is how many times you pay for it.
Inline: every call ships the full base64 (+~33%) and re-parses → simplest for a one-shot.
file_id: upload + parse once, then tiny references → wins for big or repeated documents.
Cleanup is yours: DELETE when done, or set expires_after so the server auto-deletes on a timer.
